features.summary: Broadcom reported record Q1 2025 revenue of $14.9 billion, up 25% year-over-year, with record adjusted EBITDA of $10.1 billion, up 41% year-over-year. AI revenue reached $4.1 billion, up 77% year-over-year, driven by strong demand for networking solutions and custom AI accelerators (XPUs) from hyperscale customers. The company has three hyperscale customers currently deploying XPUs at scale and four additional partners engaged in developing custom accelerators. Infrastructure software revenue was $6.7 billion, up 47% year-over-year, with VMware integration progressing well and over 60% conversion to subscription model completed.
features.management_outlook: Management expects Q2 revenue of approximately $14.9 billion with AI revenue growing to $4.4 billion, up 44% year-over-year. The company is significantly increasing R&D investment in next-generation AI semiconductors, including taping out the industry's first 2-nanometer AI XPU and developing 100 terabit Tomahawk 6 switches. Management reaffirmed their expectation that three hyperscale customers will generate a $60-90 billion serviceable addressable market by fiscal 2027, with potential for additional upside from four new partner engagements not included in this estimate.
features.risks: The company faces potential disruption from geopolitical tensions and tariff threats, though management expressed no immediate concerns about current regulations affecting their three primary AI customers. Non-AI semiconductor recovery continues to be slow, with industrial markets down double digits and enterprise networking remaining flat due to channel inventory issues. The conversion of four engaged partners to actual customers remains uncertain and could take years to materialize into meaningful revenue.
features.key_drivers: AI accelerator demand continues to drive growth as hyperscalers race toward one million XPU clusters by 2027, requiring increasingly sophisticated custom silicon solutions. The trend toward custom XPUs over general-purpose accelerators is accelerating as frontier models become more specialized and performance-critical. VMware integration is creating significant growth opportunities in private AI infrastructure as enterprises seek to run AI workloads on-premises for data sovereignty reasons, with 39 customers already adopting VMware Private AI Foundation.
